steps.instruction: Place your square paper flat on the table. Fold the left edge down along the full length of the paper to create a long vertical fold.
steps.explanation: This tall narrow arrow on the left side indicates a long downward or inward fold along the left portion of the paper, establishing the base structure.
steps.position: left-top region, spanning a tall area approximately 29x134px

steps.instruction: Fold the paper in half horizontally by bringing the right side over to meet the left side, or push the center section inward.
steps.explanation: The large wide arrow in the center-middle indicates a significant horizontal fold that brings two halves of the paper together, forming the main body shape.
steps.position: center-middle of the paper, wide arrow 104x54px

steps.instruction: Fold the top-center point or flap downward at a slight angle.
steps.explanation: This small arrow at the center-top guides you to fold a top flap or corner downward, beginning to shape the top of the model.
steps.position: center-top of the paper, small arrow 38x43px

steps.instruction: Fold the top-right corner or edge downward and inward.
steps.explanation: This arrow at the right-top area indicates folding the upper right portion of the paper down, helping to define the right side of the model's shape.

steps.instruction: Fold the right-top edge inward along its length, creating a narrow folded flap on the right side.
steps.explanation: This taller narrow arrow on the right-top indicates folding a slim strip of paper inward along the right edge, helping to taper or define the right side.
steps.position: right-top of the paper, tall narrow arrow 23x65px

steps.instruction: Fold another narrow section of the right-top edge inward or behind, similar to the previous step.
steps.explanation: A second narrow arrow near the right-top suggests folding behind (mountain fold) to create layering or to tuck an edge out of sight for a cleaner look.
steps.position: right-top of the paper, narrow arrow 24x64px

steps.instruction: On the left-middle section, gently fold or guide a small flap upward or to the side.
steps.explanation: This small arrow on the left-middle area indicates a minor adjustment fold on the left side of the model, refining the shape.
steps.position: left-middle of the paper, small arrow 12x56px

steps.instruction: Fold the top-right small corner or tip downward diagonally.
steps.explanation: This small arrow at the right-top indicates folding a small corner point to create a finished edge or a specific feature of the model.
steps.position: right-top of the paper, small arrow 25x39px

steps.instruction: Make a second small fold on the left-middle area, slightly adjusting the flap created in Step 7.
steps.explanation: A second small arrow nearby on the left-middle suggests a follow-up fold to further shape or lock the left-side flap into place.
steps.position: left-middle of the paper, small arrow 14x53px

steps.instruction: Make a small fold or tuck on the right-middle area of the model.
steps.explanation: This small arrow on the right-middle indicates a minor fold to shape or secure a flap on the right side of the model.
steps.position: right-middle of the paper, small arrow 15x33px

steps.instruction: Using the indicated fold line at the center-top, fold and unfold to create a crease reference mark.
steps.explanation: The detected fold-line symbol indicates a crease guide. Fold along this line and open it back up so the crease remains as a reference for later steps.
steps.position: center-top of the paper, fold-line symbol 79x64px

steps.instruction: Fold a small flap in the center-middle area upward or to one side.
steps.explanation: This lower-confidence arrow in the center-middle suggests a secondary fold in the middle of the model, possibly forming a detail or locking layer.

steps.instruction: At the bottom-right, fold or tuck the lower right corner inward.
steps.explanation: This arrow at the right-bottom area indicates folding the lower-right portion of the model inward to complete or close the base of the model.

steps.instruction: Make a wide, shallow horizontal fold across the center of the model.
steps.explanation: This wide but short arrow spanning the center-middle suggests a broad but shallow fold across the full width of the model, possibly flattening or shaping the midsection.
steps.position: center-middle of the paper, wide arrow 102x21px

steps.instruction: Make a final small fold in the center-middle to complete and close the model.
steps.explanation: This last arrow in the center-middle area indicates a finishing fold to lock the model's shape in place and complete the design.

notes: This diagram appears to be a beginner-level model with a moderate number of folds. Many of the lower-confidence arrows (below 0.70) may represent minor adjustment folds or could be noise in the detection. Focus on the high-confidence arrows first (steps 1-6) to establish the main structure, then apply the smaller detail folds. The single fold-line symbol suggests at least one crease-and-unfold step used as a reference guide. Work slowly and crease each fold firmly before moving to the next step.
